questions.q: The concept of alternative centres of power implies that in a country like India, power does not reside only with the political institutions. What are some other centres of power that may exist?
questions.answer: Civil society, bureaucracy, judiciary, and the economy

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of an alternative centre of power in India?
Alternative centres of power may have their own agendas and interests. What could be the implications of this for the government?
questions.answer: Alternative centres of power may not always support the government's policies, may resist changes proposed by the government, and may have their own priorities that may not align with the government's agenda.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The Indian bureaucracy is an example of an alternative centre of power because it has the power to _______________.
questions.answer: appoint and dismiss officials, collect and interpret data, and implement policies

questions.q: True or False: Alternative centres of power always work in the best interests of the people.
The judiciary is an alternative centre of power in India because it has the power to _______________.
questions.answer: interpret laws and check the abuse of power by the executive

questions.q: Civil society organisations are an example of an alternative centre of power in India. What role do they play in influencing government policies?
questions.answer: Civil society organisations play a crucial role in influencing government policies by raising public awareness, mobilising public opinion, and exerting pressure on the government to adopt policies that benefit the people.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT an alternative centre of power in India?
Alternative centres of power may have their own sources of power and influence. What are some of the factors that contribute to the power and influence of alternative centres of power?
questions.answer: Alternative centres of power may have their own sources of power and influence due to factors such as their organisational strength, financial resources, public support, and media coverage.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The economy is an alternative centre of power in India because it has the power to _______________.
questions.answer: influence government policies through economic sanctions, control over resources, and ability to create jobs
